observe: Nobody runs the moment they arrive here. You wait your turn, same as everyone else.
fault: You expected to run instantly. Look again — there's a line, and you're in it.
repair: Follow the queues. Job, ready, waiting — everyone has a place until it's their turn.
escape: Move through the queues before the window closes.

observe: Processes move between job, ready, and waiting queues before execution.
fault: A process is queued and not currently executing.
repair: Trace each queue a process passes through.
escape: Confirm the queue structure before time expires.

hints: Only the ready queue leads directly to the CPU — everything else waits first.
